results.title: Diary of a student revolution
results.creator_credit: Morten Silverstein and National Educational Television
results.brief_description: An analysis of the question whether a University administration’s action in summoning the police force was an appropriate response to peaceful demonstrations on the Connecticut University campas. Trouble began at the University when industrial recruiting of students by Dow Chemical Company resulted in a confrontation between student activists and the University President.

results.title: Diamond skull = dark obsession
results.creator_credit: Nicholas Broomfield, Tim Bevan, British Screen, Channel Four and Working Title Films
results.brief_description: Melodrama about sex and violence among the British aristocracy. Hugo, rich and titled, is insanely jealous of his business partner, Raul, whom he believes is having an affair with his wife Ginny. A drunken night turns into a nightmare and is the catalyst for further disaster. Cast includes Gabriel Byrne, Amanda Donohoe and Struan Rodger.

results.title: Dialogues with madwomen
results.creator_credit: Allie Light, Harry Saraf, Light and Saraf Films
results.brief_description: An exceptional documentary which challenges the myths, stereotypes and fears surrounding mental illness. The director herself was institutionalised for depression in the 1960s and in this film she conducts a dialogue with six other women who have all been diagnosed as mentally ill. Whether affected by schizophrenia, manic-depression or dissassociated identitities the women are all articulate and have all been able to reclaim a self-respect and self- determination that was initially denied to them by medical practitioners. This film does not claim to “solve” the riddle of madness: the women describe damage they suffered from doctors and hospitals but they also talk about the effectiveness of certain therapies and treatments. But what frustrated them all is the failure of institutionalised medicine to integrate their experiences of incest and abuse into their psychological models, or to understand the specific psychological traumas arising from racism and ethnic dislocation. The film uses symbolic imagery and dramatic re-enactments to represent the womens’ history, dreams and memories. “Dialogues with Madwomen”, by presenting the women’s own stories, tears away the heavy drapes of prejudice that dominate our thinking about mental illness.

results.title: Diablo is done for (NP)
results.creator_credit: Esben Storm, Denise Patience, Calypso Films and Dept Health and Community Services
results.brief_description: This is a drama about child sexual abuse. All is not right at Ray’s home where he is no longer able to tolerate his father’s sexual abuse of his young brother Mikey. With the support of his friends and teacher, Ray finally takes a stand against his father. This is a non-explicit film about child sexual abuse suitable for secondary students.

results.title: Diabetes - Looking After Yourself
results.brief_description: Presented by Katrina Lee, this video is designed to help people with mature onset diabetes to look after themselves. It answers the question - What is Diabetes? - and describes preventative measures such as improved diet and food care.

results.title: Development of the big man: Lucias Mitchell
results.brief_description: Basketball coach Lucia Mitchell believes that in order to bring ou the best in the big man he must be pushed harder than other players. Mitchell stress ability and reaction drills as well as ball handling skills.
